{"step_by_step_thinking": "Mycobacterium abscessus is mentioned in multiple documents as a causative agent of human disease. Document [10] explicitly states that the Mycobacterium abscessus complex bacteria (abscessus, massiliense, and bolletii) are among the atypical mycobacteria that cause infections in humans. Document [2] notes that pulmonary disease caused by Mycobacterium abscessus is resistant to most antibiotics and can only be cured in a minority of patients by surgical resection, indicating it is a pathogen that causes disease in humans. Document [7] further confirms that M. abscessus has inducible macrolide resistance and requires prolonged therapy, which is typical for a human pathogen. These findings collectively demonstrate that Mycobacterium abscessus causes disease in humans, specifically in the lungs and skin and soft tissues, and is associated with treatment challenges. Therefore, it is classified as a human pathogen.", "answer_choice": "A"}
